content.problemStatement:
You are given an undirected tree of NNN nodes. A positive weight WiW_iWi‚Äč is assigned to the node iii for all 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.

For all 1‚Č§j‚Č§N‚ąí11 \le j \le N-11‚Č§j‚Č§N‚ąí1, the jjj-th edge connects the nodes uju_juj‚Äč and vjv_jvj‚Äč, and has a restriction value of RjR_jRj‚Äč.

An array A1,A2,‚čĮ‚ÄČ,ANA_1, A_2, \cdots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚čĮ,AN‚Äč consisting of NNN non-negative integers is called **valid** if:


For all 1‚Č§j‚Č§N‚ąí11 \le j \leq N-11‚Č§j‚Č§N‚ąí1, the condition Auj+Avj‚Č§RjA_{u_j}+A_{v_j} \le R_jAuj‚Äč‚Äč+Avj‚Äč‚Äč‚Č§Rj‚Äč holds.
The **profit** of a **valid** array AAA is defined as profit(A)=‚ąĎi=1NAi‚čÖWiprofit(A)=\sum_{i=1}^N A_i \cdot W_iprofit(A)=‚ąĎi=1N‚ÄčAi‚Äč‚čÖWi‚Äč

Find the maximum possible value of profit(A)profit(A)profit(A) for some valid array AAA.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the number of nodes in the tree.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers W1,W2,‚čĮ‚ÄČ,WNW_1, W_2, \cdots, W_NW1‚Äč,W2‚Äč,‚čĮ,WN‚Äč denoting the weights assigned to every node.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contain three space-separated integers each, with the jjj-th line containing uju_juj‚Äč, vjv_jvj‚Äč, and RjR_jRj‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output the maximum possible value of profit(A)profit(A)profit(A).
constraints: 1‚Č§uj,vj‚Č§N1 \le u_j, v_j \le N1‚Č§uj‚Äč,vj‚Äč‚Č§N and uj‚Č†vju_j \neq v_juj‚ÄčÓÄ†=vj‚Äč
It is guaranteed that N‚čÖmaxi=1NWi‚čÖmaxj=1N‚ąí1Rj‚Č§1018.N \cdot \text{max}_{i=1}^N W_i \cdot \text{max}_{j=1}^{N-1} R_j \le 10^{18}.N‚čÖmaxi=1N‚ÄčWi‚Äč‚čÖmaxj=1N‚ąí1‚ÄčRj‚Äč‚Č§1018.
It is guaranteed that the input forms a valid tree for all test cases.
The sum of NNN over all test cases do not exceed 10510^5105

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case-1: All valid arrays AAA are:

A=[0,0,0]A = [0, 0, 0]A=[0,0,0]; profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=0profit(A) = 0 \cdot 6 + 0 \cdot 9 + 0 \cdot 4 = 0profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=0
A=[0,0,1]A = [0, 0, 1]A=[0,0,1]; profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=4profit(A) = 0 \cdot 6 + 0 \cdot 9 + 1 \cdot 4 = 4profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=4
A=[0,1,0]A = [0, 1, 0]A=[0,1,0]; profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+1‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=9profit(A) = 0 \cdot 6 + 1 \cdot 9 + 0 \cdot 4 = 9profit(A)=0‚čÖ6+1‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=9
A=[1,0,1]A = [1, 0, 1]A=[1,0,1]; profit(A)=1‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=10profit(A) = 1 \cdot 6 + 0 \cdot 9 + 1 \cdot 4 = 10profit(A)=1‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=10
A=[1,1,0]A = [1, 1, 0]A=[1,1,0]; profit(A)=1‚čÖ6+1‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=15profit(A) = 1 \cdot 6 + 1 \cdot 9 + 0 \cdot 4 = 15profit(A)=1‚čÖ6+1‚čÖ9+0‚čÖ4=15
A=[2,0,1]A = [2, 0, 1]A=[2,0,1]; profit(A)=2‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=16profit(A) = 2 \cdot 6 + 0 \cdot 9 + 1 \cdot 4 = 16profit(A)=2‚čÖ6+0‚čÖ9+1‚čÖ4=16

The maximum profit among all valid arrays AAA is 161616, which is the answer.
Test case-2: The optimal AAA is [9,0,0,3,1,4][9, 0, 0, 3, 1, 4][9,0,0,3,1,4].
Test case-3: The optimal AAA is [0,300000000,0][0, 300000000, 0][0,300000000,0].
